## Summary

Describe the user-facing or content change and why it is needed.

## Content evidence

- Official source URL(s):
- Academic year or intake covered:
- Date checked (UTC):
- Records changed (IDs/slugs):

Use the university, scholarship provider, or government source that owns the fact. Aggregators, blogs, and search snippets may help discovery, but must not be the sole evidence for admissions facts.

## Checklist

- [ ] I used official sources for deadlines, fees, requirements, scholarships, and application links.
- [ ] I updated `sourceIds`, `verifiedAt`, `reviewAfter`, and `status` where applicable.
- [ ] I used `null` / `not-announced` when the current cycle has not been published; I did not copy an old date into a new cycle.
- [ ] English source content is complete.
- [ ] Chinese and Russian changes were reviewed by a person; machine output is not being published unreviewed.
- [ ] I did not add passports, transcripts, contact submissions, or other personal applicant data.
- [ ] I ran `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run validate:data`.
- [ ] I checked the affected pages in the Vercel preview, including mobile and language switching.

## Reviewer notes

Call out uncertainty, temporary source outages, translation text that needs special attention, or follow-up work. Automation may report issues, but it must never decide or publish admissions facts.
